flashcards.front: What does the CAP theorem state?
flashcards.back: Distributed systems can only guarantee two of: Consistency, Availability, and Partition Tolerance simultaneously.
Strong consistency model where operations appear to execute atomically in real-time order.
Model where updates propagate asynchronously and all replicas converge to same state eventually.
Ensure consistency in replicated data by requiring majority agreement for read/write operations.
Replication technique where changes are logged before being applied to replicas.

flashcards.front: Difference between strong and weak consistency
flashcards.back: Strong guarantees immediate visibility of writes, weak allows temporary inconsistencies.
Partial ordering mechanism using (node, counter) pairs to track causal relationships.
Epidemic communication for eventual consistency through random peer-to-peer updates.
Guarantee that a process sees its own writes immediately.
Preserves cause-effect relationships between operations across nodes.
All replicas process same operations in same order using total order broadcast.

flashcards.front: What is a conflict-free replicated data type (CRDT)?
flashcards.back: Data structure designed for automatic conflict resolution in eventual consistency.
